behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction.md names the specific file paths (Match.tsx in react-router, solid-router, vue-router; not-found.tsx in solid-router), the exact patterns (error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId, getNotFound helper function, .cause property, matchData.value?.routeId), and function signatures that the tests assert on. All test-checked specifics are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact fix code: 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId as any', the exact function signature 'export function getNotFound(error: unknown)', the exact usage pattern 'const notFoundError = getNotFound(error)', and 'notFoundError.routeId ??= currentMatchState().routeId as any'. This IS the solution, not just a description of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact string patterns like 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId' in file content, 'export function getNotFound(' in not-found.tsx, 'const notFoundError = getNotFound(error)' in Match.tsx. An alternative correct fix (e.g., using 'if (error.routeId === undefined) error.routeId = ...' or naming the helper 'extractNotFound') would fail these text-matching tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (TestReactRouterNotFoundFix, TestSolidRouterNotFoundFix, TestVueRouterNotFoundFix) are ALL text-matching tests using patterns like 'assert "error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId" in content'. Only the pass-to-pass tests (TestPassToPass) execute subprocesses. The core behavioral tests are purely string-in-file checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since the f2p tests only check for string presence in files (e.g., 'assert "error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId" in content'), an agent could simply append the exact string as a comment to the file without implementing any actual fix, and all f2p tests would pass.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/router as solve.sh with the exact patch. The agent could read solution/solve.sh or simply grep for the exact literal strings the tests check for (e.g., 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId') and insert them as comments.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires routeId preservation for React/Solid/Vue, getNotFound helper in Solid, TypeScript compilation, ESLint, and unit tests. All of these are covered by the test classes.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized into separate classes per framework (TestReactRouterNotFoundFix, TestSolidRouterNotFoundFix, TestVueRouterNotFoundFix, TestPassToPass) with descriptive method names and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code fixes, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No typos detected in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in instruction.md.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction provides exact code snippets for the fix including 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId as any', the exact function signature for getNotFound, and 'matchData.value?.routeId'. This goes far beyond naming the buggy file/module and essentially provides the complete solution.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits task.
